feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Cyclospora outbreak: Hamilton County reports additional cases
entry.summary: As cyclosporiasis continues to spread to more counties and states, Hamilton County is reporting more cases.

entry.title: Can nicotine make you smarter? Why biohackers — and conservative pundits — are embracing the controversial habit
entry.summary: “Nicotine is one of the most misunderstood compounds in the biohacking world,” biohacker Dave Asprey told The Post.

entry.title: She Used Weight-Loss Drugs From a Telehealth Company, Then a GLP-1 Overdose Sent Her to the ER
entry.summary: A North Carolina woman says she was prescribed a weight-loss drug from a telehealth company and suffered a GLP-1 overdose after following their dosage instructions.

entry.title: Skin Issues Can Signal Heart Problems. Here’s the No. 1 Sign to Watch For
entry.summary: Your skin isn’t separate from the rest of your body, a dermatologist says. And it can even clue you into potential heart health issues. Here’s what to look for.

entry.title: Geriatrician reveals the habit the healthiest people over 80 always avoid
entry.summary: The healthiest people in their 80s and 90s have one surprising lifelong habit in common. Here’s why embracing the cringe may help keep your body and brain sharp.

entry.title: What is cyclospora? Diarrhoea symptoms, foods linked to US outbreak
entry.summary: Cyclospora cases are rising across the US. Here’s what to know about symptoms, spread, treatment and prevention.

entry.title: Cyclospora in Maryland: Cases more than double since last week
entry.summary: Cyclosporiasis cases in Maryland have more than doubled since last week, state health officials said.

entry.title: Ketogenic diet mediates intestinal tumorigenesis through lipids not ketones
entry.summary: A combination of different manipulations in mouse models of spontaneous intestinal adenoma formation dissects the roles of systemic and epithelial ketogenesis versus dietary lipids in ketogenic diet-mediated intestinal tumorigenesis, highlighting th…

entry.title: Biologists pinpoint how common virus triggers multiple sclerosis
entry.summary: A connection between Epstein–Barr virus and MS has long been suspected, but the underlying mechanism was unclear until now.

entry.title: Blood Test Accurately Predicts Five-Year Alzheimer’s Risk
entry.summary: The p-tau217 blood test predicts a 38% risk of cognitive impairment within 5 years.

entry.title: A Sensible New Yorker’s Guide to Legionella Season
entry.summary: With the latest Legionnaires’ outbreak in New York City, we talked to Columbia University’s Dr. Wafaa El-Sadr about whether you can drink tap water (yes) and whether you need to wear a hazmat suit walking past the Met (probably not).

entry.title: Cassidy to Trump public health nominee: ‘Why would you repeat those damn lies?’
entry.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy (R-La.), chair of the Senate Health Committee, grew angry with President Trump’s nominee to lead the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (ASPR) during his confirmation hearing on Wednesday over a misleading social media po…

entry.title: Number of UES buildings with positive Legionella tests more than doubles since preliminary findings
entry.summary: One of these buildings is likely responsible for an outbreak of Legionnaires’ disease that has sickened 60 people so far, city health officials say.

entry.title: How to protect yourself from the diarrhea-causing parasite wreaking havoc across the US
entry.summary: The parasite is borne by fresh produce and bagged salads and can cause watery diarrhea for days. Here’s what doctors say you can do to prevent getting sick.

entry.title: Cell-type signatures of Alzheimer’s disease shared across population groups
entry.summary: Single-nucleus RNA-seq and ATAC-seq analyses on post-mortem brain samples from African American, Latin and white individuals identify cell-type-specific molecular signatures that are associated with cognitive impairment and/or Alzheimer’s disease across diver…

entry.title: Ammonia pressure controls colloidal metal nitride synthesis in molten salts
entry.summary: A molten salt, high-pressure synthesis method enables solution-based production of diverse refractory metal nitride nanocrystals, expanding access to technologically important nitride materials for advanced applications.

entry.title: Modular in vivo antibody–ADC click to reverse drug resistance in tumours
entry.summary: In vivo ligation of clinically used therapeutic antibodies via bioorthogonal click chemistry to antibody–drug conjugates leads to enhanced antitumour activity in heterogeneous and treatment-resistant tumours.

entry.title: A Bayesian framework for longitudinal EHR and genetic discovery
entry.summary: A Bayesian generative framework that integrates longitudinal electronic health records with genetic data to identify latent disease signatures is presented.

entry.title: New tool makes immune therapy more effective in prostate cancer
entry.summary: Prostate cancer is often resistant to immunotherapy, which harnesses a person's immune system to recognize and destroy tumors. But a new technology that targets RNA in cancer cells gave immunotherapy new life, improving its ability to unleash cancer-killing c…

entry.title: Cancer disparities researchers say federal funding changes have disrupted their work
entry.summary: In a survey, 93% of cancer researchers who study disparities said federal policy changes have affected them. Funding is harder to come by and they worry it's slowing progress in their field.

entry.title: Could The Keto Diet Be Fueling Cancer? New Study Challenges Popular Theory
entry.summary: The ketogenic diet has become one of the world's most popular eating plans.

entry.title: Losing Just 80 Minutes of Sleep Could Cause Weight Gain, New Study Suggests
entry.summary: Losing about 80 minutes of sleep per night may raise your risk of weight gain, a new study shows. See how sleep loss affects weight and get healthy tips.

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis live updates: Diarrhea cases rise as officials hunt for source
entry.summary: Hundreds of cases of cyclosporiasis, the stomach-churning parasitic infection, have been confirmed and are under investigation.

entry.title: New cases slow in NYC Legionnaires' disease outbreak; Met museum among buildings with positive tests
entry.summary: Health officials say a Legionnaires’ disease outbreak in Manhattan’s Upper East Side now counts 60 cases, but new diagnoses are slowing. They say they are making progress on inspections for the disease-causing bacteria and found traces in dozens of buildings,…

entry.title: Medici family murder mystery may be solved after over 400 years with new DNA analysis
entry.summary: New research determined that the mysterious death of a ruler from the powerful Florentine Medici family was due to illness, not murder.

entry.title: As cyclosporiasis cases rise, consumers are left guessing which foods to avoid
entry.summary: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

entry.title: Could vitamin B3 prevent silent thief from stealing vision? New study finds protective effects against glaucoma
entry.summary: Often called the silent thief of sight, glaucoma is a group of eye diseases that gradually damage the optic nerve, often without warning signs. It is linked to increased pressure (ocular hypertension) inside the eye and, if left untreated, can lead to permane…

entry.title: 7-Day No-Sugar Anti-Inflammatory Meal Plan for High Blood Pressure, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: Inflammation plays a role in chronic conditions like high blood pressure. Enjoy this no-sugar anti-inflammatory meal plan and manage your blood pressure.

entry.title: 7 best foods to help you lose belly fat, according to dietitians
entry.summary: Spoiler: There's no magic food. But the right eating pattern, paired with a few key habits, can help reduce stubborn visceral fat.

entry.title: Man who's injected Melanotan II for years issues warning to people trying it
entry.summary: Melanotan II is a synthetic drug that darkens the skin without sun exposure.

entry.title: More health workers strike as Ebola cases in Congo exceed 2,000, including 754 deaths
entry.summary: Ebola cases in Congo have reached 2,011, with 754 deaths, according to government data. Health workers at Bunia General Hospital went on strike on Wednesday over unpaid wages. Many health professionals say they're working under difficult conditions without ad…

entry.title: Alzheimer's blood test may predict who's likely to develop symptoms in 5 to 10 years
entry.summary: A blood test may predict if apparently healthy older adults are likely to develop Alzheimer's symptoms in the next five or 10 years, researchers say.

entry.title: Parkinson’s drastically changed how they have sex. They're not alone.
entry.summary: Many couples quietly give up on sex due to Parkinson's. The American Parkinson Disease Association wants them to know they don't have to.

entry.title: If you have arthritis, these activities may lower hip-replacement risk
entry.summary: Research suggests high-impact exercises don’t damage your joints, even if you already have hip or knee arthritis.

entry.title: No, 2.8 million Americans are not "screaming and vomiting"
entry.summary: The junk marijuana stat that fooled The New York Times

entry.title: Too weak to advocate for herself, a young woman is saved by a nun
entry.summary: When Solitaire Miles was 18, she had a stroke. When she got to the hospital, doctors and nurses accused her of being on drugs. Then, her unsung hero arrived and ensured she got the care she needed.

entry.title: Healthcare Workers Are Sounding The Alarm On 28 Concerning Trends They're Seeing More Often
entry.summary: "As an American in healthcare, our food. ... It's really scary to look at young people's labs/imaging and see extreme health issues just from our average grocery store."

entry.title: Loneliness strongly linked to poorer mental health and well-being, study finds
entry.summary: People who feel lonely are much more likely to experience poorer mental health and lower well-being, a new collaborative study led by the University of Bristol, Nesta and Amsterdam UMC has found. Loneliness was also found to be linked with worse general healt…

entry.title: Under a new leader, the FDA���s biologics center moves to steady itself after stormy chapter
entry.summary: After his predecessor's turbulent tenure, Karim Mikhail is working to restore staff morale and revisit controversial rejections.

entry.title: A new biotech startup tries to tackle obesity, but not in the way you might expect
entry.summary: A new startup is making a bold move in the world of obesity drug development: It’s not working with the GLP-1 target that has taken the world by storm.

entry.title: More health workers strike as Ebola cases in Congo exceed 2,000, including 754 deaths
entry.summary: The number of confirmed cases of Ebola in Congo has reached 2,011, including 754 deaths, according to government data released overnight in what authorities...

entry.title: Mama Stray Dog Living in a Roadside Ditch With Her Only Baby Never Expected What Came Next
entry.summary: A roadside rescue gives one mama stray dog and her baby a fresh start after they were found sheltering in a ditch.

entry.title: Going to Museums and Movies May Slow Aging, Study Finds
entry.summary: Older adults who took in more culture aged more slowly, a study finds, an effect researchers compare to regular exercise.

entry.title: Patients who suffer heart attack have more micro and nanoplastic in their blood
entry.summary: People who have suffered a serious heart attack had higher levels of micro and nanoplastics in their blood, compared to patients diagnosed with chronic ischemic heart disease and those who have normal blood vessels supplying the heart, according to a study pu…

entry.title: Ebola is spreading faster in eastern Congo than it can be tracked, as deaths pass 700
entry.summary: Eighty percent of new Ebola cases in eastern Congo are emerging from unknown chains of transmission, according to WHO, a sign the outbreak is spreading faster than health officials can track.

entry.title: Study shows a blood test can help identify healthy people at high risk for Alzheimer's disease
entry.summary: A blood test that helps diagnose Alzheimer's might also forecast if healthy older adults will develop symptoms. A new study links high levels of so-called p-tau217 to a high risk of cognitive impairment in five or 10 years. Scientists stressed it's too soon t…

entry.title: Hamilton County health officials report more cyclosporiasis cases
entry.summary: As of noon Monday, Hamilton County Public Health is reporting 16 total cases, up from the 13 cases reported late last week.

entry.title: I smoked cannabis for nearly 20 years. Here’s what I wish I knew at 13.
entry.summary: As cannabis use becomes more widespread, we should be clear-eyed about some of the potential long-term effects.

entry.title: Salad greens, lettuce ‘possible source’ of cyclosporiasis parasite: Officials
entry.summary: Currently, Michigan and New York have the most cases of cyclosporiasis.

entry.title: 76 UES Buildings Test Positive For Legionella: See New List
entry.summary: Several private schools, including Spence and Dalton, are on the new list, as well as the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
